army.label: HooahHook
lines: Reflective belt at night. Red flags by day.
Cover on indoors. Feelings off until third date.
PT at zero dark thirty — availability sold separately.
CIF still has my heart. And my original issue canteen.
Cover on indoors — feelings off until the third date.
MRE heater skills transfer directly to romance.
First date at the PX food court is a power move.
Beard profile pending since before our situationship.
My mustache is unauthorized but my feelings are AR 670-1 compliant.
Women's hair: if the bun needs a second hair tie and a prayer, it's regulation-ready.
Locs, braids, twists — I need 47 photos and a commander who 'gets it' before date night.
Edge control is a readiness issue. So is texting back.
Clean shave by 0600. Emotional stubble by 0601.
If we match, I promise not to ask you to help me move until date three.
Emotional availability: same as my CIF turn-in window — technically exists.
I'm not saying I'm the best you can do, but I do know every MRE heater trick.
Relationship status: PCSing emotionally. Geographically optional.
Swipe right if you've ever called a 4-mile run 'light work' while dying inside.
My therapist said find hobbies. I chose you and the gym at 0500.
I bring: stability, a barely running POV, and strong opinions about socks.
Warning: may respond to 'hey' with a 47-minute story about a range day.
Swipe right if your beard waiver took longer than my last relationship.
My profile pic is regulation hair; my DM energy is off-duty in a hoodie.
It's a match! Time to coordinate like a convoy with no comms plan.
You both liked each other. Don't make it weird at the PX food court.
AR 670-1 wrote my personality. I'm still in appeals.
Will share a mop bucket on field day for the right person
My love language is letting you sleep through first formation
Looking for someone who understands the DFAC is a mood, not a restaurant

navy.label: FleetMate
navy.tagline: Port side for love. Starboard for lust.
lines: Liberty starts when the brow lowers. Flirting starts when Wi-Fi returns.
Underway: long distance by default. In port: short fuse by tradition.
Khakis wrinkled = personality. Uniform wrinkled = mast.
Shipmate is a term of endearment until it isn't.
Cover on indoors debate — relationship metaphor since 1775.
Galley coffee: undrinkable. My texts: same energy until port.
Clean shaven for the uniform. Scruff for the galley watch.
Women's hair: must fit under cover without looking like you fought the cover — you did.
Braids and locs? Fine — until someone says 'does that meet grooming' in the wardroom.
Facial hair waiver exists. So does my will to live. Both are rare.
I'll shave for captain's mast. I won't shave for your cousin's wedding.
Edge up fresh for inspection. Fade gone by second port call.
I'm not ghosting you — I'm just in a comms blackout. Probably.
Our first date can be anywhere with AC and a chair. I've earned it.
I have seen things. Mostly bulkhead rust and a reenlistment brief.
Swipe right if you know 'five minutes' means an hour in Navy time.
My love language is sharing my Wi-Fi password on liberty.
I will absolutely use nautical puns. You've been warned, shipmate.
Relationship goals: someone who thinks underway counts as long-distance.
Not a dependa magnet — just a person who likes clean uniforms and chaos.
My beard grows between ports. My attachment issues grow between deployments.
It's a match! General quarters for your heart.
Two hearts, one pier. Try not to make it a safety brief.
No beards at sea. Plenty of emotionally unavailable stubble ashore.
Underway schedule: chaotic. Text back: eventually.
Can explain port vs starboard using only hand gestures
Looking for someone who won't judge my rack-making standards

marines.label: SemperSwipe
marines.tagline: Every Marine a rifleman. Some of us also swipe.
lines: Every Marine a rifleman. Every date a potential field op.
Ball season: formal wear and formal lies about deployment stories.
Motivational quotes in the bio. Actual motivation: classified.
Pull-up bar in the garage. Red flag in the heart.
Cover indoors? I remove cover and expectations simultaneously.
Ball season: where dress blues meet bad decisions.
Facial hair: no. Motivation: questionable. Fade: lethal.
Women's hair: slick bun so tight it doubles as hearing protection.
If your edges aren't sharp, how sharp is your room inspection?
Ethnic hair care aisle runs longer than my enlistment — still shorter than CMC approval.
Mustache within regs or don't talk to me about discipline.
I shave with purpose. I flirt with intensity. Same blade energy.
I don't do casual — except this app. And maybe you.
If you can't handle me at my field day, you don't deserve me at my MCMAP.
Swipe right for someone who treats 'rah' as punctuation.
I'm emotionally available between 1630 and 1645 on good days.
Looking for a plus-one to the ball who won't ask what 'UA' means.
My therapist is a corporal with a white belt and opinions.
I bring intensity, a high and tight, and unsolicited motivation.
Warning: may challenge you to a pull-up contest on date two.
Looking for someone whose hair meets order and whose standards meet mine.
Oorah — it's a match. Semper maybe.
You found your fire team partner for off-base bad decisions.
High and tight isn't a haircut — it's a worldview.
Will run a PFT for your attention (bike option if feelings get real)
Motivated professional seeking motivated professional
My crayons are for sharing on the third date only

air_force.label: ChairForce Connect
air_force.tagline: Love at 30,000 feet. Feelings at desk altitude.
lines: Chair Force is a slur until you're on the bike for PT.
TDY romance: per diem includes feelings (not really).
Desk job warrior. Real job: emails about emails.
Salute indoors if you're lost. Swipe indoors if you're brave.
Waffle bar romance is real and I'm not apologizing.
TDY to your heart: per diem does not cover feelings.
Beard waiver: pending. TDY: approved. Text back: deferred.
Women's hair: neat, professional, and somehow still judged by a guy in a flight cap.
Locs and protective styles — allowed, but someone will still ask for a memo.
Clean shave for photos. Five-o'clock shadow for the debrief.
Edge up at the BX barber. Eyebrows raised at the grooming standard briefing.
My mustache is within AFI. My situationship is not.
I'm not lazy — I'm strategically conserving energy for the mission. And you.
Swipe right if you think the waffle bar is a love language.
My deployment was mostly emails. Still counts.
Relationship status: TDY to your heart. Per diem not included.
I bring: steady income, a dorm that smells like Febreeze, and memes.
First date idea: anything with AC, Wi-Fi, and no rucking.
I'm emotionally available after my nap and before my nap.
Not saying we're softer — we just prioritize ergonomics and romance.
Swipe right if you understand beard waivers are our generation's deployment patch.
It's a match! Cleared hot for feelings.
Two souls, one golf cart. Let's go.
Beard waiver submitted. Promotion board vibes.
PT test on the bike — apply within
Will trade TDY stories for coffee that isn't from a break room
Looking for someone who appreciates a good PowerPoint

space_force.label: Orbital Match
space_force.tagline: Love is the final frontier. So is this app.
lines: We're the newest branch — still explaining ourselves at Thanksgiving.
Mission: space. Location: Colorado Springs. Vibes: confused elite.
Guardian is a title. 'What do you actually do?' is a lifestyle.
Patches futuristic. Paycheck same. Ego: orbital.
Guardian beard policy orbiting a decision since 2019.
Explaining Space Force at Thanksgiving: unpaid second job.
Beard policy: ask again next fiscal year.
Women's hair: space bun literally. Figuratively also mandatory.
If your locs touch your collar, is that a grooming issue or orbital debris?
Clean shave for the official portrait. Neon scruff for the morale patch.
Edge control in zero-G is still harder than this app.
Facial hair waiver form looks like a launch checklist. Same approval time.
I'm not an alien — I just work near one on a badge.
Swipe right for long-distance that is literally orbital.
My MOS is mostly PowerPoints about space. Still elite.
First date: stargazing or the break room. Both are valid.
I bring: futuristic vibes, a cool patch, and confusion from relatives.
Relationship goals: stable orbit, minimal debris.
Not sure what we do either, but we look good doing it.
Houston, we have a match. Please don't make it awkward.
Match confirmed. Houston is mildly interested.
Two guardians, one gravity well. Don't crash.
Guardian grooming standard: inherited from Air Force, confusion included.
Guardian seeking co-pilot for terrestrial activities
Will protect your heart like it's a classified satellite
Looking for someone who gets that 'space' is mostly Colorado

coast_guard.label: Puddle Pirates
coast_guard.tagline: Not a real branch. Real matches.
lines: Not Navy. Not Army. DHS speed run.
Orange hi-vis is a love language.
Search and rescue by day. Search for texts by night.
Smallest branch, biggest 'wait you're military?' energy at bars.
Not a real branch joke — real matches though.
Orange hi-vis: visible at sea and in your DMs.
Beards allowed with waiver — romance allowed with float plan.
Women's hair: must survive SAR helmet and salt spray.
Braids under cover: practical at sea, still somehow a briefing topic.
Clean shave for uniform pics. Beard for winter small-boat ops.
Edge control vs 40-knot spray: the real readiness test.
Facial hair regs softer than Navy. Ego harder than the hull.
We're the branch everyone forgets until the hurricane. Remember me now.
Swipe right — I already have a boat. It's emotional but seaworthy.
Not Navy. Not Army. Somehow both and neither. Like my dating history.
First date: anywhere near water. Second date: explaining we're DHS.
I bring: rescue energy, orange everything, and trust issues from 'puddle pirate' jokes.
Semper Paratus — always ready. Except before coffee.
My love language is checking the weather and still showing up.
Yes, it's a real branch. No, I won't explain it again at Thanksgiving.
It's a match! All hands on deck for feelings.
Two puddle pirates, one harbor. Try not to sink the vibe.
Semper Paratus — except my beard waiver, that's still loading.
Will save you or flirt with you — Coast Guard does both
Looking for someone who respects the term 'small boat'
My schedule is SAR, paperwork, and maybe you

national_guard.label: Weekend Warrior Match
national_guard.tagline: Drill weekend is temporary. This swipe is forever-ish.
lines: M-Day ready. Date night: subject to change without notice.
Drill weekend ghosting is OPORD, not ghosting.
Same war, half the weekdays.
AT orders > your cousin's wedding. Non-negotiable.
Drill hair: regulation. Civilian hair: character witness.

Clean shave Sunday morning. Whatever happened Saturday is classified.
Women's hair: bun for formation, bonnet for the drive home.
Locs/braids approved — explain them to your civilian coworkers separately.
Beard in civilian job, baby face in uniform. Dual status energy.
Edge up before the armory. Emotional edges up never.
Grooming standard changes when the first sergeant can see you.
Sorry I missed your text — I was in the field. Or Walmart. Same energy.
Swipe right if 'I'll be free after drill' sounds romantic.
My love language is covering your shift when I don't have orders.
Relationship status: M-Day ready. Feelings: deferred to civilian time.
I bring: a real job, a military ID, and sudden availability every third weekend.
Not inactive — just aggressively scheduling around you and the armory.
First date must not conflict with annual training. Non-negotiable.
Hooah-ish. Rah-ish. Mostly 'I gotta go, first sergeant called.'
It's a match! Subject to change if orders drop.
You matched! Please confirm you're free drill weekend.
Drill weekend: regulation hair. Civilian Monday: experimental.
Civilian 28 days/month. Unavailable 2. Plan accordingly.
Looking for someone who won't panic when I say 'I have drill'
